invariants: The broker resolves the live PR head and records attempt.canonicalHeadSha during atomic prepare/start before worker start.
The broker resolves the live PR base exactly once and records attempt.resolvedBaseSha during the same transition.
Both SHA fields are full exact commit object IDs and immutable for the attempt.
attempt.canonicalHeadSha is the materialization source, ordered first parent, and expected old PR head.
attempt.resolvedBaseSha is the ordered second parent.
The runtime records the actual conflict paths and controller-selected focused tests before worker start.
Later head or base drift invalidates only this attempt and all attempt-bound results.
At most one attempt is active, under review, or eligible for promotion.

requiredProof: merge commit has exactly the attempt's two ordered parents
push uses attempt.canonicalHeadSha as expected old PR head
remote and GitHub head equal the pushed merge commit
GitHub mergeability is resolved and non-conflicting for the exact pushed head/base pair

productMechanicalGates: attempt contract, full commit object, ordered-parent, source and expected-old-head equality
fresh live head and base comparison with attempt.canonicalHeadSha and attempt.resolvedBaseSha
exact actual-conflict scope and non-conflict byte equality
zero unmerged index entries and zero unresolved conflict markers
pnpm lint:fast:files -- <exact changed TypeScript and TSX conflict paths>
pnpm exec prettier --check <exact changed text conflict paths>
exact diff, binary, symlink, secret, credential, provider/auth payload and private-path classification

comparisonPoints: immediately before broker merge construction and push

ownershipBoundary.controller: DAG, admission, checks, review policy, drift invalidation, promotion authorization and gate release

forbidden: durable packet pinning of an observed PR head or base SHA
old job, dirty worktree, patch, or attempt reuse
one-parent, squash, patch-only, reversed-parent, octopus, or whole-side promotion
repository handoff manifests or manifest hashes
real-project, team launch, product terminal, smoke, provider/auth, raw lifecycle, other-repository, broad-docs, dependency-update, or Fast activity
